plan.what: Update the public documentation and the injected agent instruction block so they explicitly require the agent to present each micro-change proposal to the user in natural prose, and so they explain that 'stepgate next' without '--suggest' only reads the currently recorded suggestion instead of creating one.
plan.why: The current behavior already treats 'next' without '--suggest' as a read-only command, but the docs leave room for confusion. The user also wants the protocol to require that agents bring the micro-step details back in prose, not only inside the CLI panel, so the written guidance needs to match that expectation.
plan.how: Revise the README cycle and command descriptions, plus the generated stepgate instruction block in init_cmd.py, to spell out both rules in plain language. Then refresh the checked-in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md blocks so the repository documentation matches what 'stepgate init' will inject going forward.
plan.expected_result: The docs will clearly say that agents must present the six proposal fields to the user in flowing prose, and that 'stepgate next' without '--suggest' only displays the current suggestion while '--suggest' records a new one.
plan.verification: Review the diff and run text searches confirming the updated docs mention the prose requirement and the read-only behavior of 'stepgate next' without '--suggest' in all affected files.

data.summary: aligned the docs so agents must present micro-change proposals back to the user in flowing prose and so stepgate next without --suggest is documented as read-only

data.evidence: Diff review confirms README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and src/stepgate/commands/init_cmd.py now state that agents must bring the six proposal fields back to the user in flowing prose, and that 'stepgate next' without '--suggest' only shows the current suggestion without changing it.

pending_suggestion: normalizar os textos mojibake no README e no bloco injetado para UTF-8 limpo, mantendo a documentacao ja alinhada com a regra de prosa e o comportamento de next
